# ClareMesh

> Financial data normalization and bi-directional sync infrastructure.

ClareMesh is an open-core platform that normalizes financial data from multiple providers (Plaid, Stripe, QuickBooks, Xero, CSV) into a canonical schema with 5 objects: Account, Transaction, Entity, Balance, and Forecast.

## Core Concepts

- Schema: 5 canonical objects with TypeScript types, Zod validation, and JSON Schema
- Transforms: Provider-to-ClareMesh normalization functions handling edge cases (Plaid sign flip, Stripe cents-to-dollars, QB JournalEntry explosion, Xero /Date() parsing)
- Sync: Bi-directional data sync between connectors with conflict resolution
- Compliance: 29 controls across 10 frameworks (SOC 2, GDPR, CCPA, PCI, SOX, PDPL, LGPD, APPI, UK-GDPR, PIPL), 17 jurisdictions

## Quick Start

```
npm install @claremesh/transforms
import { transformPlaid } from '@claremesh/transforms/plaid'
const normalized = transformPlaid(yourPlaidData)
```

## API

- Transform: POST https://ddevkorgiutduydelhgv.supabase.co/functions/v1/transform
- Schema Registry: GET https://ddevkorgiutduydelhgv.supabase.co/functions/v1/schema-registry (public, no auth)
- Compliance: GET https://ddevkorgiutduydelhgv.supabase.co/functions/v1/compliance-dashboard

## Pricing

- Open: $0/mo — 1 connector, 1K transforms/mo
- Build: $199/mo — 5 connectors, 50K transforms/mo, 4 compliance controls
- Scale: $799/mo — unlimited connectors, 500K transforms/mo, bi-directional sync, 14 controls
- Enterprise: custom — dedicated infrastructure, 29 controls, SSO, custom SLA

- Website: https://claremesh.com
- Pricing: https://claremesh.com/pricing
- Schema Browser: https://claremesh.com/schema
